title: potentilla rupestris subsp. jailae
intro: Potentilla rupestris subsp. jailae is a subspecies of the Potentilla rupestris plant, commonly known as a type of cinquefoil, which is a flowering plant in the rose family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Potentilla rupestris subsp. jailae can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
